name: Block Path Traversal and LFI Attempts
objective: Detect and block directory traversal and local file inclusion patterns in URI paths and query strings to prevent unauthorized access to filesystem resources
risk_mitigated: Local File Inclusion (LFI) allowing attackers to read arbitrary files from the server filesystem (e.g., /etc/passwd, application configs with secrets, private keys)
potential_side_effects: May block legitimate requests that include '../' in documentation paths or file browser applications. URLs like '/docs/../overview' that are structurally normalized before reaching the WAF may not be caught.
tuning_notes: Add path exclusions for legitimate file-serving endpoints. Consider switching to 'log' mode for 48 hours on applications that may use relative path syntax in APIs.
deployment_notes: Deploy in 'log' mode first. Review logs for false positives against any CMS or file browser endpoints. The encoded-dot check ('%2e%2e') is important for evasion resistance.
monitoring_notes: Path traversal attempts often come in bursts from automated scanners. Set up an alert for more than 20 blocks per IP within 5 minutes.
cloudflare_expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "../" or http.request.uri.path contains "..\\" or http.request.uri.path contains "%2e%2e%2f" or http.request.uri.path contains "%2e%2e/" or http.request.uri.path contains "..%2f" or http.request.uri.query contains "../" or http.request.uri.query contains "%2e%2e" or http.request.uri.query contains "/etc/passwd" or http.request.uri.query contains "/etc/shadow" or http.request.uri.query contains "C:\\Windows" or http.request.uri.query contains "file://")
